title: Essential Tools And File Workflows
description: Practice core RHCSA v9 command-line file operations, archives, links, permissions, and text processing.

tasks: Work in /opt/rhcsa/workspaces/essential-tools and create a tar.gz archive of the source tree named /root/essential-tools-backup.tar.gz.
Create one hard link and one symbolic link to the file data/report.txt and demonstrate that both resolve correctly.
Use shell redirection and grep to create /root/errors-only.log containing only the lines with the word ERROR from logs/app.log.
Set permissions so the directory secure is accessible by root and members of the adm group only, with group collaboration enabled on new files.
Use one command-line text-processing workflow to count how many files under data end with .conf and save the numeric result to /root/conf-count.txt.
Use system documentation to identify the tar long option for gzip compression and write only that option to /root/tar-doc-answer.txt.
hints: The workspace is already seeded for you. Focus on command-line operations, not package installs.
The secure directory task is about ownership, permissions, and the setgid bit.
A pipeline using grep, find, wc, sort, or awk is acceptable for the count task.
You can use man, info, or files under /usr/share/doc for the documentation task.
solution_outline: Use tar with gzip compression for the backup artifact.
Use ln for the hard link and ln -s for the symbolic link.
Use grep with output redirection for the log extraction.
Set group ownership to adm and apply chmod with setgid on the secure directory.
Use find plus wc or grep plus wc to derive the .conf file count.
Look up tar documentation and record the long gzip option exactly once in /root/tar-doc-answer.txt.

tasks: Create one hard link and one symbolic link for data/report.txt in the workspace.
Extract only ERROR lines from logs/app.log into /root/errors-only.log.
Restrict the secure directory to root and adm group access with collaborative group ownership behavior.
Write the count of .conf files under data to /root/conf-count.txt.
Write the tar long option for gzip compression to /root/tar-doc-answer.txt.
